summary: Plan-mode PR inventory classification for 10 listed open PRs. Six security-sensitive PRs are routed to central OpenClaw security handling. Four non-security PRs are kept independent for their own maintainer/review cycles; no close, merge, label, comment, or fix PR is planned.

actions.reason: Security-boundary-shaped memory isolation work is out of scope for ProjectClownfish cleanup and should be handled centrally.
Provider/auth and workspace-routing changes are security-sensitive under the provided preflight boundary.
Plaintext API key handling is security-sensitive and must be routed to central security handling.
Authorization behavior around isolated tool execution is security-sensitive for this worker.
Useful standalone PR with unresolved review/proof gates; not a duplicate or closure candidate in this inventory shard.
Standalone provider behavior PR with failing validation; no hydrated open canonical supports closure.
Small standalone config compatibility PR that should remain in its own review queue; draft status and proof failure block merge/closure conclusions.
Gateway endpoint exposure around agent heartbeat state is security-sensitive under the preflight boundary.
Standalone performance PR with unresolved review-bot findings and proof failure; not low-signal or closable under this job.
CI/security-analysis findings and security-sensitive preflight marking require central handling, not ProjectClownfish cleanup.
